description: data/make_data.py writes 5 of 200 'amount' values with a '$' currency prefix (e.g. '$454.75') instead of a plain number. Because pandas infers one dtype per CSV column, the presence of any non-numeric-looking value forces the *entire* amount column to be read back as strings (dtype=object/str), not just the dirty rows.
expected_failure: transform() raises pandera.errors.SchemaError: "expected series 'amount' to have type float64, got str" at RAW_ORDERS.validate(), so all three tests in tests/test_pipeline.py error out at the pipeline_output fixture. Without that schema guard, pandas.Series.sum() on the string column would silently concatenate values instead of adding them (e.g. ['337.76','69.87','15.47'].sum() == '337.7669.8715.47' instead of 423.10), corrupting totals rather than raising.
fix_hint: Strip the '$' prefix and coerce 'amount' to float during extract/parsing (e.g. pd.to_numeric after stripping currency symbols) so the column is numeric before it ever reaches RAW_ORDERS.validate() or the groupby sum.
